compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome has suffered a near-total collapse of visible biological matter. The registry-defined specimens (P1-P4) are no longer identifiable as living plants.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss (Anchor rabbit remains, plant absent)

plant_audit.P3: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen has vanished.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Desiccated, dark, and devoid of organic growth.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells in the central pot; no signs of fungal or moss activity.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no biological runoff or secondary growth.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_2: Gradual senescence observed; leaf chlorosis and postural collapse of the Pothos specimen.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_Current: Total disappearance of all foliage. The biome has transitioned from a state of decline to a state of complete biological absence.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue indicates a terminal state for all registered specimens.
anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells (likely calcium supplementation attempt)
Total loss of all plant specimens within a 5-day window
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical environment. Over the observed 5-day sequence, the specimens underwent rapid decline, culminating in the total disappearance of all plant matter. The white rabbit anchor remains, but the Pothos and other registered plants have been removed or have completely decomposed/withered away. The environment is now effectively sterile.
